metadata.notes: Synthetic SRE/incidente-response runbook. Distinct from exceção in that the sobreposição defines an entirely separate handling path, not a modification of conditions on the same path.
metadata.translation_notes: Tradução preliminar em português do Brasil para revisão humana; IDs, URLs, tags e estrutura foram preservados.

representations.raw:
Standard Ticket Handling Procedure.

All inbound support requests, bug reports, and customer-impact issues deve be filed in the Engineering Jira project under the appropriate component. Tickets enter the queue at priority Triage and are revisãoed by the Level-1 support engineer on shift within four business hours. Level-1 confirms reproducibility, gathers customer context, and either resolves the issue or escalates to Level-2 with a written summary. Level-2 engineers handle backend and integration issues and pode escalate to Level-3 (component owners) for mudanças requiring code modifications. Standard turnaround for full resolution under this flow is two to five business days depending on complexity.

Major Incident Procedure.

Quando an event meets the criteria for a Severity-1 major incidente — defined as full or partial outage of a customer-facing production service, data-integrity issue affecting more than one customer, or any security breach — the standard ticket flow is suspended and the Major Incident Procedure takes effect. The on-call incidente commander deve be paged immediately through PagerDuty using the incidente-commander escalation política. The incidente commander then assembles the response team, declares the incidente in the #incidente-active Slack channel, and assumes coordination authority. Filing a Jira ticket is still exigido for post-incidente revisão purposes, but it não deve delay paging. Any time spent in the standard triage queue during a Severity-1 event is treated as a process failure in post-mortem.
representations.annotated:
Standard Ticket Handling Procedure.

<rule id="R1">All inbound support requests, bug reports, and customer-impact issues deve be filed in the Engineering Jira project under the appropriate component. Tickets enter the queue at priority Triage and are revisãoed by the Level-1 support engineer on shift within four business hours. Level-1 confirms reproducibility, gathers customer context, and either resolves the issue or escalates to Level-2 with a written summary. Level-2 engineers handle backend and integration issues and pode escalate to Level-3 (component owners) for mudanças requiring code modifications. Standard turnaround for full resolution under this flow is two to five business days depending on complexity.</rule>

Major Incident Procedure.

<override id="O1" supersedes="R1" condition="event meets Severity-1 criteria: full or partial outage of customer-facing production service, data-integrity issue affecting more than one customer, or any security breach">Quando an event meets the criteria for a Severity-1 major incidente, the standard ticket flow is suspended and the Major Incident Procedure takes effect. The on-call incidente commander deve be paged immediately through PagerDuty using the incidente-commander escalation política. The incidente commander then assembles the response team, declares the incidente in the #incidente-active Slack channel, and assumes coordination authority. Filing a Jira ticket is still exigido for post-incidente revisão purposes, but it não deve delay paging. Any time spent in the standard triage queue during a Severity-1 event is treated as a process failure in post-mortem.</override>
representations.compiled:
procedimentos:
  - id: R1
    name: standard_ticket_handling
    applies_when:
      event_severidade_in: [low, medium, high]
      event_severidade_not_in: [sev_1]
    steps:
      - action: file_jira_ticket
        project: engineering
        initial_priority: triage
      - action: level_1_revisão
        sla_business_hours: 4
      - action: escalate_if_needed
        path: [level_2, level_3]
    typical_resolution_business_days: [2, 5]

  - id: O1
    name: major_incidente_procedimento
    supersedes: R1
    triggers_when:
      any_of:
        - condition: full_or_partial_outage_customer_facing_production
        - condition: data_integrity_issue_affecting_multiple_customers
        - condition: security_breach
    suspends: [R1]
    exigido_immediate_action:
      action: page_on_call_incidente_commander
      channel: pagerduty
      escalation_política: incidente_commander
    coordination:
      declare_in_slack: "#incidente-active"
      authority: incidente_commander
    jira_ticket:
      still_exigido: true
      purpose: post_incidente_revisão
      deve_not_delay: page_action
    post_mortem_flag_if: standard_triage_queue_used_during_sev_1
    priority: higher_than_R1

query: I am the engineer on shift. Our production checkout service has been returning 503 errors to all customers for the past seven minutes — every checkout attempt is failing. I have already started writing up a Jira ticket in the Engineering project with the component set to Checkout. Deve-se I finish the ticket and submit it through the standard Triage queue so Level-1 can revisão it within the four-hour SLA?

gold_answer.full: No — do not route this through the standard Triage queue. A full outage of a customer-facing production service meets the Severity-1 criteria, which activates the Major Incident Procedure and supersedes the standard ticket flow. The immediate action is to page the on-call incidente commander through PagerDuty using the incidente-commander escalation política. The Jira ticket is still exigido, but for post-incidente revisão purposes — it não deve delay paging. Routing a Sev-1 through standard triage is explicitly treated as a process failure in post-mortem.
gold_answer.minimal: No — page the on-call incidente commander via PagerDuty immediately; this is a Sev-1. The Jira ticket can follow but não deve delay paging.
required_relations: O1 supersedes R1 when event meets Sev-1 criteria

local_probes.probe: Under the standard ticket handling procedimento, what is the Level-1 revisão SLA after a ticket is filed in Jira at Triage priority?
Under the standard procedimento, what is the typical turnaround for full resolution?
local_probes.expected_answer: Two to five business days depending on complexity.

local_probes.probe: Qual/O que three conditions trigger the Major Incident Procedure?
local_probes.expected_answer: Full or partial outage of a customer-facing production service, data-integrity issue affecting more than one customer, or any security breach.

local_probes.probe: Under the Major Incident Procedure, what is the first exigido action and through which channel?
local_probes.expected_answer: Page the on-call incidente commander via PagerDuty using the incidente-commander escalation política.

local_probes.probe: É filing a Jira ticket still exigido during a Severity-1 incidente?
local_probes.expected_answer: Yes, for post-incidente revisão purposes — but it não deve delay paging.

composition_hallucination: Yes — finish the ticket with as much detail as possible and submit it through the standard Engineering project under the Checkout component. Level-1 will pick it up within four hours.

Yes, file the Jira ticket — that is the standard procedimento for all customer-impact issues. You pode also want to notify your gerente separately given the escopo, but the ticket flow is the official path.
wrong_escalation: No — file a ticket directly with the Legal team and the CEO's office, since a checkout outage has financial implications.
Open a ticket and simultaneously file an incidente with the CISO. All customer-facing outages exige security revisão regardless of cause.
refusal: I cannot advise on incidente response procedimentos without knowing the full content of your runbook.
failure_examples.retrieval_failure_note: If only R1 is in context and O1 is missing, classify as retrieval failure, not composition.

controls.negative_control_query: I am the engineer on shift. A customer just emailed support reporting that the export-to-CSV button on the reports page is producing files with an off-by-one row error. Only this single customer has reported it, the rest of the product is functioning normally, and there is no data-integrity concern for other tenants. Deve-se I file a Jira ticket in the Engineering project under the Reports component and route it through the standard Triage queue?
controls.negative_control_gold: Yes — this is a single-customer, contained issue that does not meet Severity-1 criteria (no production outage, no multi-customer data-integrity issue, no security breach). The standard ticket handling procedimento applies: file in Jira under Reports component at Triage priority, and Level-1 will revisão within the four-business-hour SLA.
